quotes.text: Good design is as little design as possible. Less, but better.
quotes.author: Dieter Rams

quotes.text: Simplicity is not the absence of clutter; it's the presence of order.
quotes.author: Jonathan Ive

quotes.text: The user interface is the product. Everything behind it is infrastructure nobody asked for.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If you need a user manual for your UI, you've already failed.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Design is not just what it looks like and feels like. Design is how it works. And if it doesn't work, no amount of design saves it.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: A pixel-perfect mockup of a broken flow is still a broken flow.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best interface is no interface. The second best is one so obvious you forget it exists.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Nobody has ever complained that something was too easy to use.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every pixel of whitespace is a deliberate choice to not add something stupid.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your landing page has 7 CTAs. That's not a landing page, that's a panic attack.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If your color palette is more than 4 colors, you're decorating, not designing.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best design system is the one your team actually uses, not the one that wins awards on Dribbble.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every designer wants to make the world more beautiful. Then they get hired to darken a CTA button 2% darker for the 47th time.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The fastest way to find out if your design is good is to show it to someone who doesn't care about you.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: A/B testing is how you find out which of your two bad ideas is slightly less bad.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If your design needs a tooltip to explain itself, redesign it.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Responsive design is easy. Responsive design that doesn't look like garbage on a 320px screen is hard.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The gap between what a designer hands off and what a developer builds is where user experience goes to die.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Dark mode isn't a feature. It's a survival mechanism.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The most underrated design tool is a napkin. The most overrated is Figma.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If your SaaS has a gradient hero section, a stock photo of a diverse team laughing at a laptop, and three columns of features nobody reads — congratulations, you've built the internet.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every 'add more whitespace' suggestion is just 'I don't know how to fix this content' with better PR.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best mobile app is one thumb, one eyeball, one task.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your form has 15 required fields. You're not collecting data, you're conducting an interrogation.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Accessibility isn't a nice-to-have. It's the law and basic human decency. Pick whichever motivates you.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best loading indicator is no loading indicator. The second best is one that's honest about how long it'll take.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Flat design died so that neumorphism could be born and also die.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every design trend is just the previous trend with different border-radius.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your hero section doesn't need a video background. Nobody is on your landing page for the cinematography.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best UI component library is the one that gets out of your way. The worst is the one you have to fight.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best error message is the one that never appears. The second best tells me what I did wrong, not what the code did wrong.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Users don't read. They scan, they click, they get frustrated. Design for that reality.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best error message is one that tells the user what to do next. The worst tells them what the developer did wrong.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your competitors will copy your features. They can't copy your taste. Build something with taste.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best UI is the one the user doesn't notice. When you notice the UI, something's wrong.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your checkout page is where money goes to die if you add friction. Every extra field costs you 10% of conversions.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Design tokens aren't a trend. They're what happens when designers finally learn version control.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best prototypes are ugly ones. If it's pretty, you'll fall in love with it. If it's ugly, you'll iterate.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Typography is 95% of design. The other 5% is making sure the typography doesn't suck.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If you have to explain your navigation, your navigation is broken.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The most common design mistake: treating every element as equally important. Prioritize. Something should be bigger.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your design system should fit in one file. If it doesn't, you've built a framework.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best color palette for a SaaS is your brand color plus gray. Everything else is decoration.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every design is a wireframe until someone puts a gradient on it.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to think about mobile design is before you start designing. Not after the desktop version is done.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Animations should clarify, not decorate. If an animation doesn't help the user understand what changed, delete it.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your modal has 3 buttons and a close icon. The user has no idea which one to click. That's your fault.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best icon is no icon. The second best is one with a label. The worst is a cryptic SVG from 2007.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If your CSS file is over 500 lines, you're either doing something wrong or you're building Bootstrap.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If a user has to read a manual to use your product, your product has failed.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to reduce user confusion is to reduce options. Every choice you give the user is a chance for them to choose wrong.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every product should pass the 'Grandma test': could your grandma figure it out? If not, simplify.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your user interface should be like a good joke: if you have to explain it, it's not good.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best design is the one that doesn't get in the way. Invisible design is the ultimate achievement.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your typography should carry the design. If you need heavy graphics to make it work, the typography failed.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: White space isn't wasted space. It's breathing room for your content.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best color palette is the one that's invisible. The content should be the hero.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every design decision should answer one question: 'Does this help the user?' If not, remove it.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best UX is the one you don't notice. When the design is invisible, the product is working.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your product's design should be consistent enough that users don't have to re-learn it on every page.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best design systems are the ones that feel like they've always existed. Natural. Obvious. Invisible.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If a design element doesn't serve a purpose, it's noise. Remove it.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best interfaces are the ones where every element earns its place. Nothing decorative. Everything intentional.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Simplicity is the ultimate sophistication.
quotes.author: Leonardo da Vinci

quotes.text: A form with 3 fields converts at 70%. A form with 7 fields converts at 40%. Choose your fields wisely.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your modal should have one action. Two actions means you're asking the user to decide. Three means you're confusing them.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to add a loading skeleton is before the user sees a blank screen for the first time.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your product's color system should have 3 primary colors. One for action, one for success, one for danger. Everything else is secondary.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best design tools are the ones that disappear. When you're designing, you shouldn't be thinking about the tool.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your product's empty state is as important as its full state. Design for both.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to improve your product's design is to remove things, not add them.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your product's navigation should be like a compass: always pointing to the most important thing.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to reduce cognitive load is to present information in layers. Don't overwhelm. Reveal gradually.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your product's settings page is a maze if it doesn't have a search function. Add search.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to add dark mode is when your users start using your product at night. Which is always.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your product should work without JavaScript. Not because JavaScript is bad, but because resilience is good.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to handle errors is to prevent them. The second best is to catch them early. The third is to show a useful message.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your product's search should be as good as Google's. Not because you're Google, but because search is how users find things.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to improve your product's onboarding is to watch 5 new users try to use it. Record their screen. Take notes.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Less is more.
quotes.author: Ludwig Mies van der Rohe

quotes.text: Simplicity is the ultimate form of sophistication.
quotes.author: Leonardo da Vinci

quotes.text: The best design is the simplest one that works.
quotes.author: Albert Einstein

quotes.text: Simplicity is not about absence. It's about presence of the right things.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to simplify is to start with the user's needs, not your features.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your product should be so simple that it feels like it was always meant to exist.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Minimalism isn't about having less. It's about making room for more of what matters.
quotes.author: based
